metadata.title:
LABOR BATTLE


ON IN alaska

BULLETS FLY AND MEN ARE
CIUBBED IN MINE OWNERS
CAMPAIGN TO OVERPOWER
UNION MEN.

pages.raw_text:
SEATTLE, March 1.-THE eff
forts of the Alaska mine owners to
import men to replace union miners
resulted in an outbreak yesterday
at Fairbanks in which shots were
fired but no one hurt. The arrival
of an installment of imported men
caused demonstration which a
deputy U. S. marshal attempted to
quell by using his club. One of
the miners opened fire on him,
which was returned. A number of
union men were hurt in the
clubbing.


AS a result Of the affair Tom
Steffenson. Western Federation or
ganizer; w. E. Priestley. who edits
a labor paper at Fairbanks. and
Secretary Frank Cullen, of the
union, were arrested and locked up
in default Of $4,000 bail for per-
hitting the union men to gather in
the union hall after the crowd was
broken up.


Aguarantee that the imported
men will be protected has been
given ny Z. >. MdTSudl rely, anu
the companies will continue to ship
in as many as can be secured for
the purpose of breaking down the
wage scale the union has hitherto
been able to maintain.


Advertisements for men, saying
nothing about labor trouble, have
been placed all over the states, par
ticularly in the Northwest.
